strategy: Bring your full team in from Round 1; anyone who joins after Round 2 eats Fuel and Quench.

Put a cheap Damage over Time on yourself early (anything except Fire Dragon). Every turn you take with a DoT ticking, Heidi casts a free Balanceblade on you, one per DoT. This is the fight's best exploit.
Kill each Fire Elemental yourself before it casts Raging Bull. If it dies to her side, Heidi gains a stacking +20% to +100% permanent damage aura per minion lost.
She resists Fire 70% and takes +5% from Ice. Hit with Ice or any non-Fire school. She cannot be stunned.
Once you have farmed two or three Balanceblades and are keeping the Elemental down, stack your normal blades and finish her with one big hit.
cheatNotes: Anyone entering after Round 2 gets Fuel and Quench cast on them. Start the fight with your whole team in the circle.
Timing note: if you go first, her Balanceblade lands after your turn, so plan to spend each farmed blade the following round.
Expect a Fire Elemental on Round 1 and a replacement every 3 rounds whenever one is missing. It costs her turn, not pips, so it never slows her damage.
The Elemental re-summons a replacement by casting Raging Bull. Kill it before it gets the chance.
Raging Bull is a suicide cast; the Elemental dies after using it, which counts against you and feeds her Amplify cycle.
Free blades: while any DoT except Fire Dragon is ticking on you, Heidi casts Balanceblade on you every turn you take, once per DoT. Deliberately keep a cheap DoT on yourself and farm blades.
If the Elemental dies to Raging Bull or to her side, she gains a permanent damage aura. Killing each Elemental yourself denies the whole cycle.
Defeating the minion with your own spells skips the Amplify entirely. Always take the Elemental out yourself.
Let the first Elemental die to her and she gains +20% outgoing damage for the rest of the fight.
+40% on the second. The auras stack, so her damage snowballs fast.
+100% on the fifth. With her base 135% boost her hits become lethal; never let it get here.
Cosmetic note: the auras look like Berserk, but treat them as the +damage Amplify described above.
